Mogro what grows together goes together is something the deisgners of Call of Dragons always use and again here no difference with the new infantry heroes working very well together:Madeline (See full build here ).

Garwood works with Danfel and is actually a legion pairing I like since DAnfel biggetst issue is not his kit, it’s keeping him alive so he can use his skills and Garwood increases his kit but also adds quite some suvivability to that. Run Garwood as primary commander in this legion (See full build here ).

Nika is working although there are two disclaimers here. First, you have to run Nika as the primary commander of your legion and secondaly, this is a super offensive legion dealing damage so you need another melee infantry legion to tank or they will be eliminated in a matter of seconds. I honestly wouldn’t recommend running it, but for completeness here, they work together (See full build here ).

Let’s now take a look at the numerous artifacts that you can possible use. If you really must use a PvP-focused talent tree here and you, for whatever reason, dispise the infantry tree you can potentially use this setup here that will work incredibly well dealing withother players in the open field.

Tobin is, and this is no surprise, the commander that rolls best with Urag, simply as new heroes that are released together in Call of Dragons always run best together and their kits are simply designed to work hand-in-hand where you actually get the sustainability for cavalry here that Urag needs since all his skills are pretty much about nuking in the open field (See full build here ).

Alistair so actually before you throw together two legendary cavalry commanders here -just because- you might give some thoughts into Alistair if you don’t run him with Tobin since he will give skill damage boost by percentage and also rage accumulation that do stack with Urags kit and I really like this setup although you have to face reality here with running not that much sustain and a epic commander with him. Still, akes more sense than any other weird legendary pairing with him (See full build here ).

Emrys works with Urag but full disclaimer here, they will be a extreme high-damage glass cannon and you will find yourself in so many difficult spots that I would only recommend this pairing if you have your Emrys well-built and don’t plan to build Tobin. Nothing I would plain recommend but if that is your situation this is a pairing that can make hunting in the open field exciting with the downside of the constant danger that they can also be easily taken down if you don’t pick your battles well (See full build here ).

Forondil I am not a big fan of simply as you’re missing out on the flying troops benefit here (See full build here ).

Theodore is cavalry but I honestly don’t see much synerg here other than them being both cav commanders (See full build here ).

Let’s now take a look at the numerous artifacts that you can possible use. We have tested many of them and some have amazing synergy and others are more in for the stats but no matter what artifacts you have possibly available, you will find the one that works best. Please also check out the full tier list of all artifacts here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Wolf Howl Horn are a good option if you run him with Alistair since this will trigger often enough to make a consistent increase in damage. Springblades are a good option if you run him with Alistair since this will trigger often enough to make a consistent increase in damage.

There is two possibilities here – one is running her with Maggrot in the garrison where troop types aren’t that crazy relevant but if you take her outside of any stationed structure you want archers for sure

Maggrat should be the biggest surprise here, new heroes that are released together in Call of Dragons always go exceptionally well together and so do Zayda and Maggrat and they even outclass Ffraegar with Syndrion as the top damage dealer pairing in the game now (See full build here ).

Ffraegar with the enhanced crit rate on normal attacks with all the buffs that Zayda has will work exceptionally well as well if you don’t jump the gun and get Maggrat to run along Zayda (See full build here ).

Syndrion with Zayda is also a great pairing as their kits go well together (See full build here ).

Kinnara is probably the best pairing for F2P players in Call of Dragons and their kits compliment each other really well (See full build here ).

Hosk with the awakening skill can benefit a lot from her as well and although the counterattacks are not that relevant in this pairing, it works well (See full build here ).

Kregg can be a viable early pair and although he is a flying hero, he only cares very little about flying units and you can still get away with that pair (See full build here ).

Gwanwyn can also work but away from both being markman heroes there is not a ton of synergy but if that’s your only possible pair you still do it (See full build here ).

Let’s now take a look at the numerous artifacts that you can possible use. We have tested many of them and some have amazing synergy and others are more in for the stats but no matter what artifacts you have possibly available, you will find the one that works best. Please also check out the full tier list of all artifacts here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Gilded Crossbow is the artifact you want to choose if you are running Zayda with Ffraegar and only with Ffraegar. Goldcrest is the artifact you want to choose if you are running Zayda with Ffraegar and only with Ffraegar.

With the new war pet system in Call of Dragons it’s aso important to pair Zayda with the right pet to gain more synergies. Please also check out the full tier list of all war pets in Call of Dragons here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Below you will find the talent tree builds for Zayda that make sense when running as the primary commander in any legion in Call of Dragons. Please also refer to the description below each talent tree as I explain a general route on how you should spend your talents there + some additional background for each talent build that I very likely will appreciate. For most players this is the way to go when you run Zayda as primary commander of your legion, designe to boost marksmen stats and damage as much as possible with also high mobility. You ideally want to pair her with Maggrat for this and then you can do very effective hit-and-run assaults on mages. This is the marksman talent build for her that will make her do the crazy damage when you run her with Syndrion so she can put out there with focus on that. So, if you build her to be your crazy DPS legion commander, this is the talent build to run with man focus on the marksman branch to get the main nodes before spending your points other points where it makes sense.

Goresh is so much about infantry that you want to run him with infantry.  There are some experiments to run him with cavalry but that is very specific so I don’t feel you really want to do that.

Skogul as primary commander works really well and you even get the mobility tree to be a lot more flexible in the open field and with the extra counterattack damage she eneables, this is a perfect pairing to run (See full build here ).

Garwood helps a lot keeping Goresh alive and enhancing the counterattack he deals and is also a great pairing (See full build here ).

Madeline has counterattack damage which goes so well with Goresh’s 3rd skill but the shielding can be in the way here as well. They look great on paper but the reality is you can only have one Physical ATK buff active so Madelines buff will be overwritten by Goresh’s boost so it simply disappears (See full build here ).

Bakhar is, just looking at the kit, working pretty much best with him except Skogul as you will bleed out your enemy once your health gets low – but he is an epic commander which also gets you in trouble as well when you want something strong to soak up the damage (See full build here ).

Hosk improves stats and can be a good pairing as well buffing each other up but there is not much in terms of counterattack synergy (See full build here ).

Nika can work on paper but you have some basic misconception here as you have infantry taking damage with Goresh and infantry dealing damage with Nika that makes it quite funky in the field (See full build here ).

Let’s now take a look at the numerous artifacts that you can possible use. Indis is as well as Tarra a gathering hero and both go well together, although I normally would use either of them as the primary gather heroes and run multiple gather legions (See full build here ).

Chakcha is a good gathering hero that works well with Tarra (See full build here ).

Kella is a good gathering hero that works well with Tarra (See full build here ).

Let’s now take a look at the numerous artifacts that you can possible use. We have tested many of them and some have amazing synergy and others are more in for the stats but no matter what artifacts you have possibly available, you will find the one that works best. Lucias Horn more load capacity and HP is always working on your gathering legion and works here great as well. Greenfinger Sickle more load capacity and HP is always working on your gathering legion and works here great as well.
Absolutely not needed to use a war pet on a gathering hero like Tarra.

Below you will find the talent tree builds for Tarra that make sense when running as the primary commander in any legion in Call of Dragons. Please also refer to the description below each talent tree as I explain a general route on how you should spend your talents there + some additional background for each talent build that I very likely will appreciate. This is the only talent build that honestly makes sense with Tarra – and it is for gathering. You might use her very early as supportive commander in your legion but in all honesty, don’t try to make her something she is not and build her as gathering hero and use her as that. Again, the amount of creators and sources recommending theorycraft support builds for her is out of this world. Do NOT do that with her if you play longer than 5 days on the server. Honestly, with Indis there is a way to do it but she is a legendary hero, Tarra is not. And just becuase they have similar skills and the same tree setup doesn’t mean you can just build yourself a support Tarra and be good to go. She only works as gather hero in Call of Dragons and that’s it. On top, you can pretty much max out your Indis anyway so why do that funky support healing build on Tarra?! Please, listen to me and do NOT try to make Tarra a support hero just because it’s theoretically possible.

I hope this guide for building Tarra helped you investing without wasting much time and resources.
